title: Topology Wiring Blueprint v1.2
description: Schema for a topology wiring blueprint — a pattern for arranging primitive categories into a working system shape. Each topology is a named wiring pattern with nodes, edges, constraints, and a definable parameterization surface.

topology_name.description: Kebab-case topology identifier matching the directory name

shape_description.description: Prose description of the wiring pattern: what connects to what and in what order

shape_diagram.description: ASCII art diagram showing nodes and data flow direction

nodes.description: Node definitions — each node is a role filled by a primitive from a taxonomy category

node_id.description: Short identifier for this node within the topology

role.description: The functional role this node plays in the wiring pattern. Use the most specific role available; generic roles are fallback when no specific role captures the topology-defining semantics.

role_category.description: Optional coarse grouping for the node role. Advisory metadata only; structural identity is determined by role.

primitive_category.description: The taxonomy category whose primitives can fill this role (CATEGORY_01_SOURCES through CATEGORY_06_STATE_MANAGERS)

cardinality.description: How many instances of this node the topology requires

description.description: What this node does in the topology context

edges.description: Edge definitions — directed connections between nodes with data flow semantics

data_flow.description: Push: source initiates transfer. Pull: target initiates transfer. Bidirectional: both.

delivery.description: Delivery mode when multiple targets exist

buffer_requirement.description: Whether the edge requires buffering between nodes

edge_scope.description: Whether this edge is load-bearing structural (topology-level — its removal changes the wiring pattern class) or a parameterization choice (parameterization-level — its presence or absence varies per instantiation without changing the topology class). Added in v1.1 to resolve 3-model divergence where models disagree on optional edges (dynamic registration, comparator taps, default branches).

description.description: What flows across this edge and how

constraints.description: Topology-level constraints that must hold for the wiring to be valid

state_boundary.description: Where state is held in the topology

determinism_chain.description: Conditions under which the entire topology produces deterministic output

isolation_boundaries.description: Where process/thread/sandbox boundaries must or should exist

ordering_requirement.description: Whether node execution order is constrained

failure_domain.description: How failures propagate through the topology

source_failure.description: What happens when a source node fails

transform_failure.description: What happens when a transform node fails

sink_failure.description: What happens when a sink node fails

intermediate_failure.description: What happens when an edge or buffer fails

parameterization_surface.description: What can vary when instantiating this topology for a specific domain

variable_primitives.description: Which nodes can be filled with different primitives from their category

variable_count.description: Which nodes can vary in cardinality

variable_delivery.description: Which edges can vary in delivery mode
